_meta.description: Schema for a retrieval trace record in the Oil Painting Research Assistant. A retrieval trace captures the full pipeline execution for a single query: query classification, filter construction, lexical and vector candidates, merge, reranking, diversity selection, and final context. Used for debugging, evaluation, and pipeline auditing.
_meta.storage_note: Traces are written to the metadata store on demand. Not stored in ChromaDB.

trace_id.description: Unique trace ID. Generated at trace creation time.

inferred_modes.description: Answer modes inferred from the query.

filters_applied.description: The final ChromaDB where-clause filter used for retrieval. Recorded verbatim.

lexical_candidates.description: Candidate chunks returned by BM25/lexical search before merging.

vector_candidates.description: Candidate chunks returned by ChromaDB vector search before merging.

merged_candidate_pool.description: Combined candidate pool after merging lexical and vector results. Before reranking.

sources.description: Which search legs contributed this chunk: 'vector', 'lexical', or both.

reranked_results.description: Candidate pool after reranking. Ordered by final rerank score.

selected_context_chunks.description: Final chunks selected for the context window after diversity and budget checks.

excluded_candidates.description: Candidates that were excluded from the context window and the reason for exclusion.

duplicate_suppression_notes.description: Notes on any chunks suppressed due to duplicate cluster membership.

conflict_notes.description: Conflicts detected among selected chunks that must be disclosed in the answer.

tier_coverage.description: Summary of which trust tiers are represented in the final context.

trace_timestamp.description: Timestamp when this trace was recorded.

context_package_id.description: ID of the context package produced from this trace, if assembled.

pipeline_warnings.description: Any warnings from the retrieval pipeline: empty sub-results, filter mismatches, tier gaps, budget shortfalls.
